title: Iringa Municipal Council (Council Health Service Board Establishment) Instrument, 2001
title_en: Iringa Municipal Council (Council Health Service Board Establishment) Instrument, 2001

provisions.title: Citation
provisions.content: This instrument may be cited as the Iringa Municipal Council ( Council Health Service Board Establishment) Instrument, 2001 and shall come into operation on the date of its publication in the Gazette .

provisions.title: Application
provisions.content: This Instrument shall apply throughout the jurisdiction of the Iringa Municipal Council .

provisions.title: Interpretation
provisions.content: In the Instrument unless the context otherwise requires: " Act " means the Local Government (Urban Authorities) Act; 1982 " Health Centre Committee " means a committee established under section 16 of this Instrument. " Health Centre Management Team " means a team of head of section or units of a health centre ; " Ward Development Committee " means a committee established under section 15 of the Local Government (Urban Authorities) Act ; [ Act No. 8 of 1982 ] " Dispensary Committee " means a committee established under section 27(1) of this Instrument; " Council Health Management Team " means a technical health manage­rial organ of the district established under section 13 of this Instru­ment; " dispensary " means a primary care facility, which offer out patient services, including maternal and child health services, and may have up to four beds for observation of patients for not more than 24 hours; " district " means and includes a city, municipal or town; " health centre " means a health facility usually located at the word level, offering outpatient and inpatient health care services including ma­ternal and child health services with a bed capacity of about 15 beds; " hospital " means a level 1 - hospital establishment in the municipal empowered to supervise all health services in the district ; " designated hospital " means a voluntary agency level 1 - hospital estab­lishment impowered to act as a Municipal hospital on contractual agreement with the Ministry; " health facility " means and includes a hospital , health centre , medical and dental clinics, disensary, pharmacy, health laboratory, diagnos­tic centre, maternity home nursing home and such other facilities established or organised for purposes of delivering health services; " Medical Officer of Health " means a medical practitioner who is the officer in-charge of health services in the Municipal; public health facility " means a health facility that is mainly owned and managed by the Government to address people's needs; " Minister " means the minister responsible for Local Government and Regional Administration. " Non Governmental Organisation (NGO) health facility " means a health facility owned, financed and run by an organisation or an associa­tion. Normally is charitable non profit making with the aim of complimenting public or private services and operates within laid down regulations of the Government; " private health facility " means a health facility owned and managed by an individual or corporate body mainly for profit; " Board " means the Council Health Service Board established under section 4(1) of this Instrument; [ Act No. 8 of 1982 ] " Council " means the Municipal Council established under section 5 of the Local Government (Urban Authorities) Act , 1982; " Regional Health Management Team " means a managerial organ estab­lished by the Ministry of Health; " Ward Health Committee " means a committee established under section 23 of this Instrument. [ Act No. 8 of 1982 ]

provisions.title: Establishment of the Board
provisions.content: (1) There is hereby established a Board , to be known as the Coun­cil Health Service Board . (2) The offices of the Board shall be within the Municipality.

provisions.title: Composition of the Board
provisions.content: (1) The Board shall be composed of the following members:— (a) Selected members:— (i) four community service users of health facility of whom at least two shall be female; (ii) one representative each from a non proft voluntary agency and a private for profit health care facility, appointed by the Council from amongst health care facilities which have en­tered into a service agreement with the Board ; (iii) the chairman of the Council Education, Health and Economic Affairs Committee; (b) Secretarial:— (i) the Municipal Economist; (ii) the Medical Officer of Health who shall be the Secretary to the Board ; (iii) one representative from the hospital ; and (iv) one representative from the Regional Health Management Team , (2) The Chairman shall be elected from amongst members specified under paragraph (a) of subsection (1) of this section. (3) Members specified under paragraph (a) other than members speci­fied under paragraph (b) of subsection (1) , shall have voting rights in any decision of the Board.

provisions.title: Tenure of office and mode of selection
provisions.content: (1) Members of the Board shall hold office for a period of three years from the date of their nomination and cease to be a member after expiry of that period but shall be eligible for re-nomination for another term of three years. (2) At the end of the tenure of office of the current Board , the Council Director shall announce to the public the open vacancy, which require competent personnel to fill them by filling special application forms and submit them to the Council . (3) If any vacancy occurs in the membership of the Board by the reason of death, resignation, permanent incapacity or any other reason, another person shall be nominated by Council on recommendation of the Board to fill up the vacancy and person so nominated shall hold office for the remaining term, provided that, nothing in this section shall be construed as allowing to nominate another person if the remaining period is less than six months. (4) The person so nominated as per subsection (3) above shall be eligible for re-election. (5) Selection of application shall be made by the Full Council through the Education, Health and Economic Affairs Committee, Finance and Administration Committee after being scrutinized by the Council Man­agement Team. (6) The Board shall be approved by the full Council and inaugurated under the witness of the District Commissioner. (7) If the Board fails to fulfill its expected obligations as stipulated by the relevant Council 's Instrument the Council shall reprimand or dis­solve the Board and arrange for an immediate selection.

provisions.title: Meetings of Council Health Service Board
provisions.content: (1) The Board shall ordinarily meet quarterly in every calendar year and the Chairman shall convene all meetings of the Board . (2) The Chairman may convene an extra-ordinary meeting of the Board or request in writing signed by not less than one third of the total number of members of the Board when the need arises;

provisions.title: Quorum and voting at meeting
provisions.content: (1) A half of the total number of members in office shall consti­tute a quorum at any meeting of the Board . (2) A decision of the majority of members present and voting at a meeting shall be deemed to be a decision of the Board ; (3) Secret vote system shall be used in Board 's meetings; (4) Every member of the Board shall have on vote and in event of equality of votes, the Chairman of the meeting shall have a second cast­ing vote in addition to his deliberative vote.

provisions.title: Qualification of members
provisions.content: (1) The qualification of Board members shall be as follows:— (a) he is a Tanzanian citizen; (b) for representative of NGOs and Voluntary Agency, the question of citizenship shall not apply; (c) he has attained the age of 25 years and not older than seventy years, provided that the subject is of sound body and mind; (d) Applicants shall have at least completed secondary education and above; and (e) for selected members save for the chairman of education; Health and Economic Affairs Committee, shall not hold any political post. (2) A councilor or an employee of the Council , whether elected or not, Save for Chairman of Education; Health and Economic Affairs Committee shall not be allowed to be a voting member of the Board .

provisions.title: Roles and Functions of the Board
provisions.content: (1) The roles, functions and responsibilities of the Board shall be as follows:— (a) to ensure that the population receives appropriate, affordable, promotive, preventive, curative and rehabilitative health care services; (b) to discuss, amend health plans, budget and submit to the Council for approval; (c) to receive, analyse and approve implementation reports from the Council Health Management Team ; (d) to identify, mobilize and solicit financial resources for running Council health services; (e) to mobilise adequate resources that will ensure effective, effi­cient and equitable access to health services in its area of juris­diction; (f) to support Council Health Management Team in managing and administering health resources; (g) to prepare and recommend on a Council wide health plan and budget the committee responsible for health services; (h) to promote sustainable health infrastructure and reliable logis­tics and supply systems; (i) to develop, supervise and manage health services in its area of jurisdiction.

provisions.title: Relationship of the Board with community, stakeholders and other authorities
provisions.content: Relationship of the Board with community, stakeholders and other authorities shall be as follows:— (a) the Board shall ensure full involvement of the local community in priority identification, planning, financial contributions, monitoring and evaluation of health services delivery; (b) the Board shall be accountable to the Council whereby:— (i) the Council Health Plans, quarterly, annual technical and finan­cial reports shall be reviewed by Health, Education and Economic Af­fairs Committee and Finance and Adminstration Committee and approved by Full Council ; (ii) The Board shall conduct its activities in such a way that it shall maximize the efficiency of the Council and its existing organs without Council jeopardizing the Board 's autonomy. (c) the Council Health Management Team shall be a technical structure for implementing decision passed by the Board . (d) in respect of Education, Health and Economic Affairs Committee:— Committee:— (i) all plans and progress reports shall pass, therefore, this Com­mittee will facilitate relationship. (ii) close collaboration with other sectors will be actively searched by the Board towards the implementation of joint efforts in priority areas within the Council . (e) with Non Governmental Organizations, private providers and donors:— (i) Members representing the Voluntary Agency, Non Govern­ment Organizaitons and private providers in the Board will facilitate the relationship; (ii) The Board will require a proof from Council Health Manage­ment Team that other stakeholders have been involved in the planning process; (iii) The Board shall solicit funds from all various Sources including Council , Voluntary Agency, Community and donors. (g) the Regional Health Management Team shall provide technical support and advisory role; [please note: numbering as in original.] (h) the Ministries responsible for Regional Administration Local Government and Health shall give technical support to the Board by formulating policies, regulatory framework and monitoring of the Board 's activities.

provisions.title: Source and management of funds
provisions.content: (1) The Source of funds for the Board shall be:— (a) community contributions; (b) Community Health Funds; (c) cost sharing and user charges fees; (d) Councils revenue collection; (e) donors and partners (f) Central Government grants; and (g) other sources legally acquired. (2) There shall be a single health services account to be known as account number 6, which shall follow Local Government financial regu­lations, and signatories shall be as prescribed from time to time. (3) The Board shall maintain fund account for the purposes of Com­munity Health Fund initiatives. The account will maintain revenues from sales of Council Health Fund cards and collection of user fees from Council Health facilities. (4) The account shall be used to contribute resources to Account number 6 of the Council in financing health services. (5) Signatories of this special account shall be as stipulated in the by law establishing Community Health Fund. (6) The Council shall appoint or assign two accountants who shall oversee health accounts under the Medical Officer of Health . (7) The Medical Officer of Health shall initiate and originate all pay­ments. (8) Both accounts shall be audited following the prevailing local government financial regulations.

provisions.title: Establishment of Council Health Management Team
provisions.content: There shall be established for each district , the Council Health Management Team .

provisions.title: Composition of the Council Health Management Team
provisions.content: The Council Health Management Team shall consist of:— (a) Medical Officer of Health ; (b) Nursing Officer; (c) Municipal Health Officer; (d) Municipal Pharmacist; (e) Municipal Medical Laboratory Technologist, and (f) Municipal Dental Surgeon

provisions.title: Roles and Functions of the Council Health Management Team
provisions.content: The Roles and functions of the Council Health Management Team shall be:— (a) to prepare comprehensive district health plans in line the district health needs and National District Health Planning Guidelines. (b) to ensure that hospital , health centres, dispensaries implement health activities, community owned resource persons and the commu­nity as per comprehensive district health plan. (c) to ensure the provision of transport, drugs, vaccines, medical sup­plies, equipment and reagents to hospitals, health centres and dispensa­ries. (d) to respond to epidemics, emergencies and plan for control and preventive measures accordingly; (e) to carry out supportive supervision to district health staff at all levels in the district ; (f) to ensure that quality health services are provided in line with national standards: (g) to ensure health legislation, regulations, professional ethics and codes of conduct are adhered to in the district ; (h) to ensure that data collection, analysis, utilisation and feedback are used by health workers to plan and implement proper interventions; (i) to identify priority areas, organise and conduct operational re­search in the district ; (j) to compile and analyse quarterly and annually all financial re­ports and submit them to the Council Health Service Board and to the Regional Health Management Team ; (k) to organise meetings of all key health partners operating in the district in order to identify' and coordinate all health activities; (l) to organise and support outreach health services; and (m) to monitor and evaluate implementation of health activities in the district ;

provisions.title: Establishment of a Health Centre Committee
provisions.content: (1) There shall be established for each health centre , a Health Centre Committee which shall be composed of: (a) three members appointed from different wards within the area where circumstances allow. (b) one member appointed by the Council from the Dispensary Com­mittee (on rotational basis); (c) one member appointed by private for profit health facility in the area; (d) the officer in-charge of the health centre who shall be the Secretary; (e) one member from the Ward Development Committee ; and (f) one member representing private not for profit health facility . (2) Health Centre Committee may invite other persons who are not members to provide expertise and other assistance but such invited persons shall not take part in the final decisions of the Committee. (3) At least one third of the members shall be women. (4) A Chairman of Health Center Committee shall be selected from Amongst its members.

provisions.title: Qualification for membership
provisions.content: A person shall be qualified to be a member of a Health Centre Committee if he satisfies the following conditions:— (a) he is a citizen of United Republic of Tanzania; (b) he has attained the age of twenty one years and not older than sixty five years; (c) he is ordinarily resident within the area; (d) the level of his education is primary school or above, and can read and write in Kiswahili; and (e) he does not hold any political post.

provisions.title: Tenure of office of members and made of selection
provisions.content: (1) The term of office of a member shall be three years and all the members shall retire on the third anniversary of the date on which they assumed their offices and if not re-elected for another term of three years only, newly elected members shall come in office. (2) Any appointed member may resign by giving a written notice of one month to the Chairman. (3) Where a member resigns or dies the vacant post shall be filled and the appointed member shall hold office only for the remainder of the term of his predecessor but shall be eligible for re-election. (4) If the Health Centre Committee fails to fulfil its expected obliga­tions the Council shall reprimand or dissolve the Committee and ar­range for an immediate election. (5) The list of elected candidates shall be forwarded to Ward Devel­opment Committee for final selection after being scruitinised by Ward Management Team.

provisions.title: Meeting of Health Center Committee
provisions.content: (1) The Committee shall hold ordinary meetings for the trans­action of its business at least once in every three months at a place, date and such time as it may decide provided that, the Committee may con­vene an extra ordinary meeting as it may deem fit. (2) The Chairman of the Committee may convene an extra ordinary meeting of the Committee after the receives a written request to that effect signed by not less than one third of all tire members of the Com­mittee stating clearly tire purposes for which the extra ordinary meeting is to be convened. (3) The secretary shall, not less than twenty four hours before the time appointed for the holding of a meeting of the Committee, notify every member in writing of the place, day and time of the meeting and of the business proposed to be transacted at the meeting.

provisions.title: Functions of a Health Centre Committee
provisions.content: (1) It shall be the duty of every Committee of a Health Cen­tre:— (a) to receive and discuss implementation reports prepared by the Health Centre Management Team ; (b) to liaise with the Council Health Board and other partners in health provisions and promotion in its area; (c) to promote sustainable health infrastructure and reliable supply or drugs and logistic systems; (d) to advise and recommend to the Council Health Board on human resources development in terms of recruitment, training deployment and motivation at the Health Centre level; (e) to facilitate the Health Centre Management Team in planning and managing community based health initiatives within its catchment area in the context of the Ward Development Plan; (f) to promote availability of adequate health centre infrastructure; (g) to oversee the availability of essential supplies of drugs, reagents and medical equipment. (2) The Health Centre Committee shall be responsible and accountable to the Council for its day to day functions.

provisions.title: Funds and source of revenue
provisions.content: (1) Funds and sources of revenue of a Health Centre Commit­tee shall constitute the following:— (a) community contributions; (b) donors or partners donations; (c) Government grants; (d) District Council 's grants; (e) any other source legally acquired. (2) There shall be a special account at a bank recognized by the Coun­cil where all the funds for a health centre shall be deposited. (3) There shall be two signatories for cheque of a health centre . The Officer in-charge of a health centre shall be category "A" and a user of a Health Centre shall be category "B"; (4) There shall be in every health centre a strong room and a safe for the custodian of money. (5) The health centre account shall be audited in accordance with the local government financial regulations. (6) The health centre shall send quarterly, biannual and annual progress reports to the Council Health Service Board to be represented before the full Council meetings.

provisions.title: Relationship with other sectors
provisions.content: (1) The Health Centre Committee shall, in performance of its functions:— (a) make sure that the community needs are addressed; (b) solicit funds from various sources; (c) keep and maintain a Health Fund Contributor's register; (d) develop and strengthen partnership for collaboration, coordina­tion and intergration with other sectors in the area; (e) make sure that the Dispensary Committee is represented in the Health Centre Committee ; and (f) make sure that the Health Centre Committee is represented in the Council Health Service Board . (2) The Council Health Management team shall give technical ad­vice to the Health Centre Management Team .

provisions.title: Establishment of the Ward Health Committee
provisions.content: There shall be established a ward Health Committee.

provisions.title: Composition of the Ward Health Committee
provisions.content: (1) The Ward Health Committee shall be composed of:— (a) the Councilor of the respective ward; (b) the ward Executive Officer; (c) one head teacher from a primary school located in the ward who shall be appointed by the Ward Development Committee , on his ab­sence a Ward Education Coordinator shall act accordingly. (d) two members from the community elected by the community of which, one of whom shall be a female; (e) a Clinical Officer or an Assistant Clinical Officer in charge of a health care facility who shall be the Secretary to the Committee, or health officer or health assistant where there is no a dispensary ; (f) one member appointed by the Ward Development Committee from amongst persons proposed by the mitaa within the area of that ward; and (g) one representative from a community based organization appointed by the Ward Development Committee . (2) A person shall be eligible to be a member of a Ward Health Com­mittee if:— (a) he is citizen of the United Republic of Tanzania; (b) he has attained the age of twenty one years and not older than sixty five years; (c) he has a primary education and can read and write in swahili; and (d) save for a councillor, he does not hold any political elected post through any political party. (3) Tenure of office and mode of selection of the Ward Health Com­mittee shall be as in the Health Centre Committee , by involving respec­tive organs of the ward. (4) At least one third of the members shall be women; (5) Chairman of the Ward Health Committee shall be selected from amongst its members.

provisions.title: Functions of Ward Health Committee
provisions.content: Ward Health Committee shall have the following functions:— (a) to mobilize the community to be members of the Community Health Fund; (b) to prepare the list of members and monitor the number of mem­bers in the community (c) to supervise the collectors of annual contributions;- (d) to monitor the level of contributions and user fee revenues; (e) to reveiw Community Health Funds operations, make recommen­dations and take remedial actions; (f) to initiate and coordinate comprehensive community health plans; (g) to organise general meetings of members of the Community Health Fund.

provisions.title: Source of funds
provisions.content: Source of funds for a Ward Health Committee shall be:— (a) Community Health Fund collections; (b) community contributions; (c) donors and partners donations; (d) Council 's grants; and (e) any other source legally acquired.

provisions.title: Established of a Dispen­sary Committee
provisions.content: (1) There is established a committee to be known as a Dispen­sary Committee. (2) A Dispensary Committee shall be composed as follows;— (a) selected members who shall be vote members:— (i) three members from community dispensary users; (ii) one represantative from private not for profit health facility ; (iii) one member representing private for profit health facility . (b) non-vote members:— (i) One representative from a Ward Development committee; (ii) one representative from a mtaa; and (iii) in-charge of the dispensary who shall be the Secretary. (3) At least one third of the members shall be women. (4) A Chairman of the Dispensary Committee shall be selected from Amongst its selected members.

provisions.title: Mode of selection and Tenure of Office
provisions.content: (1) Members of a Dispensary Committee shall be selected through the following procedure:— (a) Ward Executive Officer shall advertise the vacant post for repre­sentation of Health facilities users; (b) the list of elected candidates shall be forwarded to the Ward Management Team for scruitiny before being forwarded to Ward Devel­opment Committee which shall do the final selection; and (c) the final selection shall be forwarded to the Council for approval and inform accordingly the Ward Development Committee . (2) Tenure of Office for Dispensary Committee members shall be three years but may seek re-election for another term of three years onlv

provisions.title: Meetings of Dispensary Committee
provisions.content: (1) A Dispensary Committee shall meet quarterly, but it may convene an extra-ordinary meeting when a need arise. (2) Fifty percent of the selected members together with a Chairman or Secretary shall form a quorum for any meeting of a Dispensary Com­mittee.

provisions.title: Qualification of members
provisions.content: A person shall be eligible to be a member of a Dispensary Com­mittee if:— (a) he has attained the age of twenty one years and not older than sixty five years; (b) he has completed primary education or above and can read and write in Kiswahili; (c) he is a Tanzanian and ordinarily a resident of a particular Coun­cil. The condition of citizenship shall not apply to a person who is work­ing in a voluntary health facility ; (d) he is a representing the users of a dispensary , (e) he does not hold any political post.

provisions.title: Functions of a Dispensary Committee
provisions.content: A Dispensary Committee shall have the following functions:— (a) to ensure that the population in its area of jurisidiction re­ceives appropriate and affordable health care services; (b) receive, discuss and pass the dispensary plans and budget; (c) receive the implementation report prepared by Dispensary Man­agement Team; (d) identify and solicit financial resources for running dispensary services; (e) liaise with other health boards and partners in health provision and promotion; (f) promote sustainable health insfrastructure and reliable supply and logistic system; (g) advise and recommend on human resources concerning re­cruitment, training, selection and deployment to relevant authorities; (h) assist and facilitate the Dispensary Managment Team in plan­ning and managing the community based health initiatives within its chatchment area in the context of the Ward Development Plan; (i) submit quarterly, biannual and annual reports to the Council Health Service Board ; and (j) do any relevant acitivity as may be directed by the Ward De­velopment Committee.

provisions.title: Relationship of Dispensary Committee with relevant Stakeholders
provisions.content: For the better performance and efficiency in its day to day activities a Dispensary Committee shall liaise with the following:— (a) community, by ensuring that the community contribute mem­bers to its committee to ensure that community needs are adequately addressed and facilitate flow of information form both ways; (b) Council Health Managment Team, which shall give technical advice to it; (c) the Council Health Service Board , by being represented in it on a rotational basis to ensure that dispensary concerns are addressed; (d) the Dispensary Management Team, to ensure that the team implementes Council 's decisions; (e) the Health Centre Committee , by being represented in order to ensure that dispensary concerns are addressed; (f) other stakeholders, by soliciting funds, develop and strenghthen partnership for collaboration, co-ordination and integration.

provisions.title: Sources of funds
provisions.content: Sources of funds for a Dispensary Committee shall include but not limited to the following:— (i) community contributions; (ii) Community Health Fund; (iii) Councils funds (iv) funds from donors or pamters; (v) Government Grants; and (vi) other sources legally acquired. The Common Seal of the Iringa Municipal Council is hereunto af­fixed in pursuance of the Resolution passed at a meeting of the said council duly convened and held on the 4 th September, year 2001 and the same was affixed in the presence of:— ____________________________ Mayor ____________________________ Municipal History of this document 04 January 2002 this version Published in Gazette of the United Republic of Tanzania 1 Commenced 05 November 2001 Assented to Related documents More documents like this one [] [] [] [] ▲ To the top > Contact us Visit our Twitter/X profile Visit our Linked In profile Visit our YouTube channel About TanzLII TanzLII is operated by the Judiciary of Tanzania and the Office of the Attorney General, Chief Parliamentary Draftsman, and publishes the law of Tanzania for free online access for all.
